tweets.text:
@USBPChiefEPT made an arrest of an illegal alien attempting to cross the border into the United States. After agents made the arrest, it was uncovered that he had a prior conviction for forcible touching and sexual contact of a victim under the age of 13. 

There is no way of knowing who comes across our borders without catching them and fully vetting them, but our agents hold the line to protect this country from intruders. This illegal alien will be charged with felony re-entry and deported back to his home country.
tweets.text_resolved:

card.name: summary_large_image
card.title: DHS Seizes 660 Pounds of Meth From Commercial Truck at Border
card.description: Only days before the methamphetamine seizure, the agency took possession of $6.8 million worth of cocaine at the same port of entry.

author.description: The official page of United States Customs & Border Protection. https://t.co/dOTgpZSOKF

tweets.text:
Another excellent seizure by West Palm Beach’s Trade Enforcement Team, with assistance from the Center of Excellence and Expertise Apparel, Footwear and Textiles team, West Palm CBP Research and Intelligence Operations Team (RIOT), and Homeland Security Investigations (HSI). 

The operation resulted in the arrest of an individual on felony charges for possessing and intending to distribute over 25 counterfeit items. Additionally, two illegal aliens were identified by officers and transported to U.S. Border Patrol West Palm Beach Station for processing.

The estimated Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) of the seized counterfeit merchandise, had it been genuine, exceeds $1,500,000.00 USD. Distribution of counterfeit products is not a victimless crime, CBP is committed to protecting the American public from dangerous and illicit goods.
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
United States Border Patrol Agents in Del Rio arrested an illegal alien with prior convictions of illegal re-entry, indecency with a child, and aggravated sexual assault of a child. This illegal alien now faces the maximum sentence, 20 years.

United States Attorney Simmons says it best, "I hope the message is now clear, but if not, I’ll say it plainly. If you’re an illegal alien that has committed the types of heinous offenses that were committed by the defendant in this case, we will hold you to account.”

Read the whole story here: https://t.co/LxX1dcy3gN

tweets.text:
Brownsville @CBPSouthTexas officers seized a load of cocaine valued at almost $500K. Officers discovered 16 hidden packages within the vehicle. CBP officers removed the packages which contained a total of 36lbs of alleged cocaine.

Read: https://t.co/eJqRse30yO https://t.co/Mw5q4VK3Ch

tweets.text:
Counterfeit items may seem enticing due to their low prices and easy access, but they are not the best option. 

In January, CBP officers in Pittsburg seized over $300K of knockoff designer goods. The parcel from China contained high-end designer brand products, bearing the brand names Chanel, Christian Dior, Fendi, Louis Vuitton, and Van Cleef & Arpels.

tweets.text:
Thinking about heading home? Now’s the perfect time!

Use the CBP Home App to self-depart and you could receive a $2,600 exit bonus—up from $1,000!
That’s enough for a first-class ticket, souvenirs, AND a selfie with your boarding pass.
Don’t miss out—leave with cash and a smile! 😄✈️💸

@DFODetroit
#SafeJourney #BonusUpgrade #CBPHome #SelfDeport

tweets.text:
It’s 100 days until the U.S. hosts #FIFAWorldCup! Along with @WHTaskForceFIFA,  here’s what CBP is doing to ensure a successful tournament:
•Inspecting all cargo that enters the stadiums
•Ensuring travel is safe and legal
•Combatting counterfeits
•Conducting Airspace security

#100DaysToFWC26 #WeAre26
